channel.title: Azure｜企業テックブログRSS
channel.description: Azureセクションのブログ更新をまとめたRSSフィード

item.title: The Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent’s Playbook: A Practical Livestream Series for Building Better Agents | Microsoft for Developers
item.description: Building on Microsoft 365 Copilot? Here’s your playbook. Declarative agents are quickly becoming one of the most exciting ways to extend Microsoft 365 Copilot and bring organizational knowledge, workf
item.content:encoded: Building on Microsoft 365 Copilot? Here’s your playbook. Declarative agents are quickly becoming one of the most exciting ways to extend Microsoft 365 Copilot and bring organizational knowledge, workflows, and tools directly into the flow of work. But as agent capabilities grow, so does the need for practical guidance: How do you build agents that […]The post The Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent’s Playbook: A Practical Livestream Series for Building Better Agents appeared first on Microsoft for Devel
item.author: Shavonna Jackson

_custom.originalTitle: The Microsoft 365 Copilot Agent’s Playbook: A Practical Livestream Series for Building Better Agents

item.title: AT&amp;T and Microsoft scale trillion-token workloads with Microsoft Foundry and AMD | Microsoft Azure Blog
item.description: AT&amp;T processed approximately one trillion tokens while developing OTel2.0 using Microsoft Foundry Managed Compute, open AI models, and AMD and NVIDIA GPU infrastructure. Discover how flexible model ch
item.content:encoded: AT&amp;T processed approximately one trillion tokens while developing OTel2.0 using Microsoft Foundry Managed Compute, open AI models, and AMD and NVIDIA GPU infrastructure. Discover how flexible model choice and scalable infrastructure are enabling production-scale telecom AI.The post AT&amp;T and Microsoft scale trillion-token workloads with Microsoft Foundry and AMD appeared first on Microsoft Azure Blog.
item.author: Steve Sweetman

_custom.originalTitle: AT&T and Microsoft scale trillion-token workloads with Microsoft Foundry and AMD

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure DDoS Protection custom policy | Azure service updates
item.description: Azure DDoS Protection custom policyis now in public preview. This capability provides per-resource control overDDoS mitigation thresholds for protected Standard Load Balancer frontend IPconfigurations
item.content:encoded: Azure DDoS Protection custom policyis now in public preview. This capability provides per-resource control overDDoS mitigation thresholds for protected Standard Load Balancer frontend IPconfigurations. Customers can configure fixedinbound detection th

_custom.originalTitle: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure DDoS Protection custom policy

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Standard service endpoint | Azure service updates
item.description: Standard service endpoint is now available in public preview, introducing a scalable and secure way to connect IaaS workloads to Azure PaaS services under the Private Link family. This enhanced capabi
item.content:encoded: Standard service endpoint is now available in public preview, introducing a scalable and secure way to connect IaaS workloads to Azure PaaS services under the Private Link family. This enhanced capability overcomes the scale and management limitations of

_custom.originalTitle: [In preview] Public Preview: Standard service endpoint

item.title: How to test agent experience changes without shipping them | Microsoft for Developers
item.description: Most changes you think will improve AI agent behavior won't. We tested a dozen hypotheses on a real project upgrade scenario and the majority failed. Learn how to emulate documentation, API, and MCP s
item.content:encoded: Most changes you think will improve AI agent behavior won't. We tested a dozen hypotheses on a real project upgrade scenario and the majority failed. Learn how to emulate documentation, API, and MCP server changes locally so you can validate what works before shipping anything to production.The post How to test agent experience changes without shipping them appeared first on Microsoft for Developers.
item.author: Waldek Mastykarz, Garry Trinder

_custom.originalTitle: How to test agent experience changes without shipping them

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Protect sensitive generative AI telemetry in Application Insights and Microsoft Foundry | Azure service updates
item.description: Azure Monitor Application Insights now stores generative AIcontent in a dedicated GenAIContent table (AppGenAIContent in LogAnalytics), making it possible to apply newlyavailable access controls to se
item.content:encoded: Azure Monitor Application Insights now stores generative AIcontent in a dedicated GenAIContent table (AppGenAIContent in LogAnalytics), making it possible to apply newlyavailable access controls to sensitive AI telemetry. Because ApplicationInsights p

_custom.originalTitle: [In preview] Public Preview: Protect sensitive generative AI telemetry in Application Insights and Microsoft Foundry

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway | Azure service updates
item.description: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway enables customers to run IPv6 inner traffic through VPN tunnels in a dual‑stack configuration. IPv6 is supported on all production SKU gateways that use standard pub
item.content:encoded: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway enables customers to run IPv6 inner traffic through VPN tunnels in a dual‑stack configuration. IPv6 is supported on all production SKU gateways that use standard public IPs.IPv6 inner traffic can be configured for both n

_custom.originalTitle: [Launched] Generally Available: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Python 3.14 | Azure service updates
item.description: You can now develop functions using Python 3.14 locally anddeploy them to Azure Functions plans on Linux. Upgrade your apps to Python 3.14 to take advantageof enhanced security, a longer support windo
item.content:encoded: You can now develop functions using Python 3.14 locally anddeploy them to Azure Functions plans on Linux. Upgrade your apps to Python 3.14 to take advantageof enhanced security, a longer support window, and continued compatibility withthe Azure Functio

_custom.originalTitle: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Python 3.14

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Functions Support for PowerShell 7.6 | Azure service updates
item.description: Azure Functions support for PowerShell 7.6 is now available in preview. You can now develop apps using PowerShell 7.6 locally and deploy them to Azure Functions plans. Learn more: What'snew in PowerSh
item.content:encoded: Azure Functions support for PowerShell 7.6 is now available in preview. You can now develop apps using PowerShell 7.6 locally and deploy them to Azure Functions plans. Learn more: What'snew in PowerShell 7.6 Azure Functions PowerShell versions and their

_custom.originalTitle: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Functions Support for PowerShell 7.6

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Defender security assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server | Azure service updates
item.description: Microsoft Defender Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server are now generally available. You can use assessments to continuously evaluate
item.content:encoded: Microsoft Defender Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server are now generally available. You can use assessments to continuously evaluate the security posture of your Azure PostgreSQL flexible

_custom.originalTitle: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Defender security assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Encryption in Transit for Azure Files NFS Shares in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) | Azure service updates
item.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now generally supports Encryption in Transit (EiT) for Azure Files NFS v4.1 volumes through the Azure File CSI driver. With EiT enabled, data transferred between AKS wor
item.content:encoded: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now generally supports Encryption in Transit (EiT) for Azure Files NFS v4.1 volumes through the Azure File CSI driver. With EiT enabled, data transferred between AKS workloads and Azure Files NFS shares is encrypted using TL

_custom.originalTitle: [Launched] Generally Available: Encryption in Transit for Azure Files NFS Shares in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)

item.title: How to test agent skills without hitting real APIs | Microsoft for Developers
item.description: Your agent skill calls an API. The moment you start evaluating it, every run either costs money or mutates production data. Learn how to mock APIs transparently so you can run evals without changing y
item.content:encoded: Your agent skill calls an API. The moment you start evaluating it, every run either costs money or mutates production data. Learn how to mock APIs transparently so you can run evals without changing your skill or hitting real endpoints.The post How to test agent skills without hitting real APIs appeared first on Microsoft for Developers.
item.author: Waldek Mastykarz

_custom.originalTitle: How to test agent skills without hitting real APIs
